Because the visible explanation may not be the **only** explanation.

A cephalhaematoma **plus** undiagnosed G6PD deficiency or ABO incompatibility produces a far higher level than either alone.

>> Finding a plausible cause is not the same as excluding the dangerous ones.

---

## Step 7: Why follow up for anaemia?

Blood in the swelling is blood **not in the circulation**.

As it resorbs over the following weeks, the baby may become anaemic.

Iron is also being sequestered and slowly recycled.
cards.tie:
This case connects three parts of the lecture at once:

- The birth history
- The examination for trauma
- The Coombs-negative aetiology column

And it makes a nice point: sometimes the cause of neonatal jaundice is visible on the **outside** of the skull.

cards.q: KEY CUES — What words, findings and timings should immediately point you towards each cause of neonatal jaundice?
cards.lecturer: Summary of the discriminating features drawn from the aetiology, history and examination sections of the lecture.
cards.explain:
## How to use this card

In an exam you rarely have time to reason from first principles.

You **recognise the pattern** first, then justify it afterwards.

These are the phrases that should make a diagnosis jump off the page.

---

## Timing cues

**"Jaundice in the first 24 hours"**

→ **ALWAYS pathological.** Think haemolysis: ABO, Rh, G6PD, congenital infection.

---

**"Jaundice beyond 14 days"**

→ Prolonged jaundice. Think biliary atresia, hypothyroidism, UTI, galactosaemia, breast milk jaundice.

---

## Maternal and blood group cues

**"Mother is blood group O"**

→ **ABO incompatibility.** IgG anti-A/anti-B crosses the placenta. Hits first pregnancies. **Coombs positive.**

---

**"Mother rhesus negative" · "previous affected baby" · "no anti-D given"**

→ **Rh isoimmunisation.** Worse with each pregnancy. **Coombs positive.**

---

## The G6PD cluster

**"Male baby"** + **"camphor/naphthalene balls"** · **"herbal preparation"** · **"sulfa drugs"** · **"fava beans"**

→ **G6PD deficiency.** X-linked. **Coombs NEGATIVE.**

Film: **bite cells, blister cells, Heinz bodies.**

>> Remember: the assay can be falsely normal during a crisis.

---

## The obstruction cluster

**"Pale/clay stool"** + **"dark urine"** + **"greenish or muddy yellow"** + **"past 14 days"**

→ **Conjugated hyperbilirubinaemia → BILIARY ATRESIA until proven otherwise.**

Surgical emergency. Refer before 8 weeks.

>> **Never give phototherapy.**

---

## The two breastfeeding pictures

**"Thriving, exclusively breastfed, after day 7, peaks 2–3 weeks"**

→ **Breast MILK jaundice** — but only after excluding conjugated causes and hypothyroidism.

---

**"First week"** + **"weight loss over 10%"** + **"dehydrated"** + **"glucose water given"** + **"milk not come down"**

→ **BreastFEEDING jaundice.** Treat with **MORE** feeding.

---

## The sepsis cluster

**"Home delivery"** + **"offensive/discharging umbilical stump"** + **"fever or hypothermia"** + **"refusal to feed"** + **"vomiting"** + **"abdominal distension"**

→ **Sepsis.**

>> Sepsis both haemolyses **and** impairs the liver — and makes bilirubin more toxic at any level.

---

## The birth trauma cluster

**"Vacuum or forceps"** + **"prolonged second stage"** + **"boggy swelling that does NOT cross sutures"**

→ **Cephalhaematoma.**

- Crosses sutures **and present at birth** → caput
- Crosses sutures, diffuse, shocked baby → **subgaleal — dangerous**

---

## Two prolonged-jaundice look-alikes

**"Constipation, coarse features, large fontanelle, umbilical hernia, hypothermia"**

→ **Congenital hypothyroidism.**

---

**"Vomiting after milk feeds" + "hepatomegaly" + "cataracts" + "reducing substances in urine"**

→ **Galactosaemia.**

---

## The brain cues — know these cold

**"High-pitched cry" + "poor suck" + "lethargy" + "lost Moro"** (days 1–3)

→ Acute bilirubin encephalopathy, **PHASE 1**.

---

**"Opisthotonus" + "retrocollis" + "rigidity" + "oculogyric crisis" + "fever" + "seizures"** (middle of week 1)

→ **PHASE 2.**

>> Exchange transfusion immediately — whatever the number.

---

**"Choreoathetosis" + "upward gaze palsy" + "sensorineural deafness"**

→ Established **chronic kernicterus**.

---

## Two quick visual cues

**"Palms and soles yellow"** → Kramer zone 5 → **15–20 mg/dL or more.** Measure and treat now.

**"Bronze-grey discoloration on phototherapy"** → **bronze baby syndrome** → the jaundice was conjugated. **Stop the lights.**
cards.tie:
This is your fast-recall layer over the whole lecture.

Recognise the pattern first.

Then use the reasoning from the other cards to justify it.
